title: Accessible Names, Images, Language, Headings, Landmarks, and Human Review · Program Brief: Page Language Title
summary: The tool counts alt attributes, assumes heading rank alone proves structure, ignores accessible names and language, and labels automated checks WCAG conformance. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the page language title increment in accessible page-quality audit with human-review handoff. Learners progress through prediction, e…
objectives: Check declared page and part language, document title presence and purpose, and conflicts with observed content while retaining human-review limits.
Distinguish syntax, schema, visible-content, vendor-eligibility, policy, rendering, and live search evidence and avoid ranking or display guarantees.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-accessibility-audit-4
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-accessibility-audit-4.
# Case 1655: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def worked_scraper_accessibility_audit_4(pages: int = 24, automated_findings: int = 38, manual_checks: int = 42, conformance_claims: int = 0, findings_with_evidence: int = 38) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "authorizationworked4",
        "url1655",
        "response4dekrow",
        "treeworked41655",
        "frontier1655worked4",
        "report4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or automated_findings < 0:
        return False, pages, "accessibility-count"
    if findings_with_evidence != automated_findings:
        return False, findings_with_evidence, "finding-evidence"
    if manual_checks < pages:
        return False, manual_checks, "manual-review-gap"
    if conformance_claims > 0:
        return False, conformance_claims, "conformance-overclaim"
    assert findings_with_evidence == automated_findings and conformance_claims == 0
    return True, automated_findings, "bounded-accessibility-audit"
